data.title: PDS Biotechnology Price Target Maintained With a $21.00/Share by HC Wainwright & Co.
Advanced Biomed Closes $6.6 Million IPO
data.description:
Advanced Biomed said Friday it has raised about $6.6 million from the initial public offering of 1.6 million shares at $4.00 per share.

The biotechnology company said it has granted its underwriter a 45-day option to buy up to 246,000 additional shares. Craft Capital Management acted as underwriter for the offering.

The shares started trading on Nasdaq Thursday.

data.title: Pfizer Appoints Ex-J&J Leader to Accelerate Weight Loss Solutions
data.description:
Pfizer (PFE, Financial) is stepping up its obesity drug development by naming James List as its new chief internal medicine officer, according to a Friday press release. The former Johnson & Johnson (JNJ, Financial) executive brings extensive expertise from his post?doctoral research in GLP?1 weight loss drugs.

Based in New York, the pharmaceutical giant is refocusing its R&D efforts to fast?track development of cardiovascular, metabolic, hematologic, and obesity treatments. List previously led global therapeutic initiatives at J&J's Janssen unit, emphasizing his background in managing complex drug portfolios.

The move comes as Pfizer intensifies competition in the obesity market, currently dominated by Eli Lilly (LLY, Financial) and Novo Nordisk (NVO, Financial). A recent TD Cowen note indicated that the duopoly is expected to persist until 2030. In 2023, Pfizer discontinued its twice?daily oral obesity regimen due to adverse side effects, shifting attention toward a once?daily formulation now in late?stage development.

The appointment is part of Pfizer's broader strategy to enhance its anti?obesity portfolio and secure a stronger global market presence.

data.title: Update: Market Chatter: Trump Could Impose Reciprocal Tariffs on Canada as Soon as Friday
data.description:
(Updates with additional details throughout the story.)

The United States may impose matching tariffs on Canadian lumber and dairy products as early as Friday to match Canada's current tariffs on US goods, Bloomberg reported Friday, citing US President Donald Trump.

"They'll be met with the exact same tariff unless they drop it, and that's what reciprocal means," Trump reportedly said in the Oval Office.

His remarks reportedly followed a decision to delay the planned 25% tariff on certain Mexican and Canadian products until April.

Trump wants to address what he calls Canada's restrictive practices on US lumber and dairy, the media outlet reported.

The administration is preparing a system to apply certain tariffs on various countries, mirroring each nation's tariffs or other barriers on American products, according to Bloomberg.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Powell Says Fed Focused on 'Separating the Signal from the Noise' in Trump Era — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Megan Leonhardt

Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said Friday that the U.S. economy remains in a good place. But policymakers are holding steady as they wait for greater clarity on the effects of the Trump administration's numerous policy changes on the economy.

In prepared remarks set to be delivered at the 2025 U.S. Monetary Policy Forum in New York City, Powell acknowledged that he and other Fed officials are carefully monitoring the effects of the Trump administration's policy changes in regard to trade, immigration, fiscal policy, and regulation.

The White House has moved forward on some policies, such as implementing higher tariffs on goods from China, but has also backtracked on others, such as tariffs on Mexico and Canada. Powell said that the uncertainty around such changes and their likely economic impacts remains "high." As such, he believes that the Fed doesn't need to move quickly to adjust policy in response yet.

"It is the net effect of these policy changes that will matter for the economy and for the path of monetary policy," Powell said. "As we parse the incoming information, we are focused on separating the signal from the noise as the outlook evolves. We do not need to be in a hurry, and are well positioned to wait for greater clarity."

Powell added that monetary policy is not on a "preset course." If the economy remains strong but inflation doesn't continue to move sustainably toward 2%, he expects the Fed can keep interest rates steady. But if the labor market deteriorates or inflation cools more quickly than anticipated, Powell said that the Fed can easily lower rates accordingly. "Our current policy stance is well positioned to deal with the risks and uncertainties that we face in pursuing both sides of our dual mandate."

While the 151,000 jobs added in February was weaker job growth than economists expected to see, Powell largely shrugged off the latest signs of softening labor conditions. "Many indicators show that the labor market is solid and broadly in balance," Powell noted, saying that employers have added a solid monthly average of 191,000 jobs since September.

The unemployment rate remains low at 4.1%, and while wages are outpacing inflation, they're at a more sustainable pace than earlier in the pandemic recovery. "With wage growth moderating and labor supply and demand having moved into better balance, the labor market is not a significant source of inflationary pressure," Powell added.

Powell also reiterated that while the Fed remains focused on bringing inflation back to its 2% target, he's not overreacting to a couple of higher prints.

"The path to sustainably returning inflation to our target has been bumpy, and we expect that to continue," Powell said. "Inflation can be volatile month-to-month, and we do not overreact to one or two readings that are higher or lower than anticipated."

In the coming months, Powell said he expects to see progress in housing services inflation as well as non-housing services.

Powell also noted that officials began their second five-year review of monetary policy framework at the January Federal Open Market Committee meeting. He reiterated that the 2% inflation goal will be retained and won't be a focus of the review, though policymakers will consider the consensus statement — which includes the statement on longer-run goals and monetary policy strategy — and central bank communications.

"We will consider lessons of the past five years and adapt our approach, where appropriate, to best serve the American people, to whom we are accountable," Powell said, noting that he expects the review to be finalized by late summer.

Today's remarks are Powell's final opportunity to provide some public context on how policymakers are weighing the shifting economic landscape, including significant trade policy updates, persistent inflation, and waning consumer sentiment. The Federal Open Market Committee is set to meet on March 18-19, and the central bank's blackout period starts on Saturday.

Traders currently see a 97% chance that the central bank keeps the fed-funds rate steady at the conclusion of the March policy meeting, according to CME's FedWatch tool.

Organized by the University of Chicago Booth School of Business, the annual conference aims to bring together policymakers, leading economic scholars, and market analysts to discuss U.S. monetary policy. The focus of this year's meeting is a new report titled "Monetary Policy Transmission to Real Activity" produced by a panel of scholars.

Before Chair Powell's keynote presentation, Governor Michelle Bowman and New York Fed President John Williams joined discussions on the latest research. Former Cleveland Fed President Loretta Mester will lead a panel discussion on central bank framework reviews in the afternoon.

Write to Megan Leonhardt at megan.leonhardt@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Market Chatter: Visa Asked by Democratic Lawmaker to Hand Over Records of Payments Deal With X
data.description:
Visa has been asked to hand over records of its payments deal with Elon Musk's X by Sen. Richard Blumenthal, CNBC reported Friday, citing a letter it obtained.

Blumenthal, D-Connecticut, the ranking member of the Senate's Permanent Subcommittee on Investigations, reportedly sent a letter to the company Thursday asking for detailed plans and documents on the companies' deal.

Visa struck a deal with X in January to be the social media company's first partner for X Money, its built-in payments system.

X Money will debut later this year and will allow for funding to a user's X Wallet via Visa Direct. It will also connect to a user's debit card, allowing peer-to-peer payments, and provide the option to instantly transfer funds to the user's bank account.

Blumenthal reportedly raised conflict of interest concerns over Musk's role at the Department of Government Efficiency and his position as X chairman. DOGE recently trimmed headcount at the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, X Money's main regulator, and Visa stands to take advantage, Blumenthal reportedly said.

Visa did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Market Chatter: Mexico Says Up to 90% of Exports to US May Get Tariff Relief
data.description:
Mexico's government said that up to 90% of its exports to the US may enjoy relief from tariffs following US President Donald Trump's decision Thursday to exempt Mexican and Canadian goods covered by the United State-Mexico-Canada trade agreement until April 2, Bloomberg reported Friday.

(Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.)

data.title: Canada to Offer C$6.5 Billion to Help Firms Mitigate Tariff Fallout
data.description:
By Paul Vieira

OTTAWA--The Canadian government started to roll out fiscal support to minimize the damage from U.S. tariffs on companies and workers.

At a press conference, officials said Friday the government would make available 6.5 billion Canadian dollars, or the equivalent of C$4.6 billion, in financing to help affected companies. The bulk of the financing, C$5 billion, is to help exporters find new, non-U.S. markets to sell their goods.

Canada is also making changes to its jobless-benefit plan, which makes payments to workers who lose work. It is making changes to help employers keep workers while reducing their hours because of trade disruption.

"We're taking a calibrated response to address the uncertainty and damage this trade war brings and the risks it is creating for our businesses, our workers and all Canadians," said Canadian Employment Minister Steve MacKinnon.

MacKinnon added it is providing this support even though President Trump issued another one-month reprieve on 25% tariffs on nonenergy Canadian imports, and 10% on energy products. "We know that uncertainty in many ways is worse than the proposals themselves, because businesses are frozen in their investment decisions and other issues."

Write to Paul Vieira at paul.vieira@wsj.com

data.title: CAVCO Industries Director Sells 4,000 Shares
data.description:
Reporter NameGreenblatt David A.
RelationshipDirector
TypeSell
Amount$2,115,520
SEC FilingForm 4

David A. Greenblatt, a Director at CAVCO Industries, sold 4,000 shares of Common Stock on March 5, 2025, for a total sale amount of $2,115,520. The sale was executed at a weighted average price of $528.88 per share. Following this transaction, Greenblatt directly owns 15,286 shares of CAVCO Industries. The sale was part of an automatic trading plan under Rule 10b5-1, established on December 4, 2024.

SEC Filing: CAVCO INDUSTRIES INC. [ CVCO ] - Form 4 - Mar. 07, 2025

data.title: Walgreens mega-LBO tests ‘better in private’ maxim
data.description:
After a decade of failing to solve fundamental problems, the drugstore chain is a mess. A $10 bln buyout makes the usual promise that issues are best fixed out of the public eye. The first benchmark: selling cash-burning primary care. Investors seem to have reasonable doubts.

Full view will be published shortly.

Follow @rob_cyran on X

CONTEXT NEWS

Walgreens Boots Alliance said on March 6 it had agreed to sell itself to Sycamore Partners for approximately $10 billion, or $11.45 per share, a 29% premium to the stock's price prior to media reports of a potential deal. Shareholders will also receive a non-transferable right to receive up to $3 a share from the future monetization of Walgreens' debt and equity interests in primary care provider VillageMD.

The company said the total transaction value is up to $23.7 billion based on the immediate cash consideration, potential value from the divestiture, net debt, capital leases, and present value of certain legal liabilities, less the fair value of equity investments.

As part of the deal, Executive Chairman Stefano Pessina will reinvest his 17% stake in the company.

data.title: Wolfspeed jumps after lowering capital expenditures forecast, job cuts announcement
data.description:
** Shares of chipmaker Wolfspeed rise 12.3% to $5.64

** WOLF is reducing its headcount by about 180 employees, primarily in materials operations, at locations in Durham and Siler City

** Company now expects fiscal 2026 capital expenditures of about $150 mln to $200 mln vs. prior expectations of $200 mln to $600 mln

** Targets fiscal 2027 capital expenditures of about $30 mln to $50 mln vs. analysts' average estimate of $325.2 mln, according to data compiled by LSEG

** Up to the last close, stock had fallen ~81% over the past 12 months

data.title: REG - Next PLC - Transaction in Own Shares
data.description:
RNS Number : 8995Z Next PLC 07 March 2025  

NEXT plc

For immediate release

07 March 2025

Transaction in Own Shares

NEXT plc announces that, under the irrevocable, non-discretionary programme to purchase shares for cancellation announced on 28 January 2025, today it purchased on market 11,223 of its ordinary shares of 10p each (the "Shares"), all for cancellation, at a price of 9789.7038 pence per Share.

The highest price paid per Share was 9940p and the lowest price paid per Share was 9704p.

These purchases were made through the Company's broker UBS AG London Branch.

Following the purchase, the Company's registered share capital consists of 123,333,353 Shares.  This figure may be used by shareholders to determine if they are required to notify their interest, or a change to their interest, in NEXT plc under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

All of the Shares have equal voting rights and there are no shares held in Treasury.

S Anderson

Transaction in own shares

The Company announces that on 7 March 2025 it purchased for cancellation 34,744 of its ordinary shares of 5.6110477936 pence each ("Shares") through Barclays Bank plc, in accordance with the authority granted by shareholders at the Company's Annual General Meeting on 6 September 2024.

Date of transaction:                                                        7 March 2025

Highest price paid per Share:                                         3,642p

Lowest price paid per Share:                                         3,566p

Volume weighted average price paid per Share:           3,610.0013p

The Shares purchased on 7 March 2025 will be cancelled and will not be held as Treasury shares. Following the cancellation of these Shares, the Company has 100,080,441 Shares in issue (excluding Treasury shares). This is the figure which should be used by shareholders as the denominator when determining whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in the Company under the Financial Conduct Authority's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

data.title: Will AI Demand Push Broadcom Stock Beyond $1 Trillion Again?
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI)-powered demand drove Broadcom stock’s market cap beyond the $1 trillion mark. However, macroeconomic uncertainties and concerns over a potential slowdown in AI infrastructure investments recently led to a pullback in its stock price.

Despite these concerns, Broadcom’s latest earnings report for the first quarter of its fiscal 2025 reignited investor enthusiasm, as the company reported solid financials. The semiconductor giant posted total revenue of $14.9 billion, marking a 25% year-over-year increase, surpassing analysts’ expectations. Its consolidated adjusted EBITDA also hit a record high of $10.1 billion, up 41% year-over-year.

Active Investor: FREE newsletter going behind the headlines on the hottest stocks to uncover new trade ideas

In response to this strong performance, Broadcom’s stock surged approximately 10% in the pre-market session on Friday, March 7, signaling renewed confidence among investors.

AI-Fueled Semiconductor Growth

Broadcom’s AI-driven semiconductor revenue was the highlight of its earnings report, with total semiconductor revenue reaching $8.2 billion, an 11% year-over-year increase. The key driver of this growth was AI-related revenue, which soared 77% year-over-year to $4.1 billion, exceeding management’s initial guidance of $3.8 billion. This outperformance was attributed to stronger-than-expected shipments of networking solutions to hyperscalers that are rapidly expanding their AI capabilities.

Looking ahead, Broadcom expects momentum in AI-related revenue to continue. For Q2, the company has projected AI revenue to reach $4.4 billion, reflecting a 44% year-over-year increase. This robust growth trajectory reflects the increasing dependence of hyperscalers on Broadcom’s high-performance accelerators and networking products to support their AI workloads.

Broadcom’s management remains optimistic about AI's long-term potential, highlighting that its three largest hyperscaler customers will generate a serviceable addressable market (SAM) of $60 billion to $90 billion by fiscal 2027. Additionally, the company is actively working with two other major hyperscalers to develop customized AI accelerators, further expanding its market reach.

Challenges in Non-AI Semiconductor Segments

Broadcom’s AI-driven revenue is soaring, but its non-AI semiconductor business is facing some turbulence. In the first quarter, revenue from non-AI semiconductors came in at $4.1 billion, marking a 9% decline from the previous quarter. This dip was largely due to seasonal weakness in the wireless segment, while other areas of the business have been slow to recover.

There are, however, some bright spots. Broadband showed strong double-digit growth in Q1, and that momentum is expected to continue into the second quarter. Server storage, another key segment, is also on the path to recovery, with projections pointing to high-single-digit growth in Q2. On the other hand, enterprise networking remains sluggish as customers work through existing inventory, keeping a lid on overall growth in the non-AI space.

Despite these challenges, Broadcom’s booming AI business is more than making up for the weakness elsewhere. AI demand is set to drive a 2% sequential increase in semiconductor revenue in Q2, translating to an impressive 17% year-over-year gain. While non-AI segments may take some time to rebound, the company’s strength in AI continues to be a major growth engine.

Expanding Software Revenue Through VMware Integration

Beyond semiconductors, Broadcom’s Infrastructure Software segment has become an increasingly important revenue driver, particularly following its acquisition of VMware. In Q1, Infrastructure Software revenue soared to $6.7 billion, up 47% year-over-year, benefiting from deal timing and the transition from perpetual licenses to subscription-based revenue models. As of Q1, over 60% of VMware’s licenses had transitioned to subscription-based models, significantly enhancing Broadcom’s recurring revenue stream.

A key growth driver in Broadcom’s software business is VMware Cloud Foundation (VCF), which enables enterprises to virtualize their entire data centers. Around 70% of Broadcom’s largest customers have adopted VCF, creating further opportunities for upselling and expansion.

Additionally, the rise of enterprise AI has opened new avenues for growth, as Broadcom’s VMware Private AI Foundation — developed in collaboration with Nvidia — has already attracted 39 enterprise customers. This platform enables enterprises to virtualize GPUs alongside CPUs, optimizing AI workloads for cost efficiency and performance.

For Q2, Broadcom expects Infrastructure Software revenue to be $6.5 billion, reflecting a 23% year-over-year increase, reinforcing the strength of this segment.

Will Broadcom Stock Break the $1 Trillion Mark?

Broadcom’s robust AI-driven growth and expanding software footprint will support its growth. The company has already breached the $1 trillion market cap once, and given its robust financial performance, it could do so again.

That said, risks remain. Macroeconomic uncertainties and a potential slowdown in AI infrastructure spending could impact growth. However, Broadcom’s strong guidance and continued expansion into high-demand markets suggest it has plenty of runway ahead.

Wall Street analysts remain overwhelmingly bullish on AVGO, with a consensus rating of “Strong Buy.”

On the date of publication, Amit Singh did not have (either directly or indirectly) positions in any of the securities mentioned in this article. All information and data in this article is solely for informational purposes. For more information please view the Barchart Disclosure Policyhere.
